잠언 2:1.content: My son, if you will receive my words, and store up my commandments within you,

잠언 2:2.content: so as to turn your ear to wisdom, and apply your heart to understanding;

잠언 2:3.content: yes, if you call out for discernment, and lift up your voice for understanding;

잠언 2:4.content: if you seek her as silver, and search for her as for hidden treasures:

잠언 2:5.content: then you will understand the fear of Yahweh, and find the knowledge of God.

잠언 2:6.content: For Yahweh gives wisdom. Out of his mouth comes knowledge and understanding.

잠언 2:7.content: He lays up sound wisdom for the upright. He is a shield to those who walk in integrity,

잠언 2:8.content: that he may guard the paths of justice, and preserve the way of his saints.

잠언 2:9.content: Then you will understand righteousness and justice, equity and every good path.

잠언 2:10.content: For wisdom will enter into your heart. Knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.

잠언 2:11.content: Discretion will watch over you. Understanding will keep you,

잠언 2:12.content: to deliver you from the way of evil, from the men who speak perverse things,

잠언 2:13.content: who forsake the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness,

잠언 2:14.content: who rejoice to do evil, and delight in the perverseness of evil,

잠언 2:15.content: who are crooked in their ways, and wayward in their paths,

잠언 2:16.content: to deliver you from the strange woman, even from the foreigner who flatters with her words,

잠언 2:17.content: who forsakes the friend of her youth, and forgets the covenant of her God;

잠언 2:18.content: for her house leads down to death, her paths to the departed spirits.

잠언 2:19.content: None who go to her return again, neither do they attain to the paths of life.

잠언 2:20.content: So you may walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.

잠언 2:21.content: For the upright will dwell in the land. The perfect will remain in it.

잠언 2:22.content: But the wicked will be cut off from the land. The treacherous will be rooted out of it.
